nodes.label: AI as writing and grammar assistant
nodes.grounding_span: ai is really good for all the grammatical aspects of my job

nodes.label: AI as task delegator for complete tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI as requiring excessive prompt engineering
nodes.grounding_span: It saves me time being able to delegate this side of my work

nodes.label: AI as tool for cultural sensitivity and inclusivity
nodes.label_negative: AI as culturally Americanized and irrelevant

nodes.label: AI as efficient quiz/test creator
nodes.label_negative: AI as missing the sweet spot of difficulty and engagement

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings in lesson planning
Culturally relevant and engaging education
nodes.grounding_span: things applicable to the nationality and cultural context of my students

nodes.label: Professional autonomy
nodes.grounding_span: I am not worried about ai taking over for a while

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for simple syntax issues
nodes.grounding_span: grammar tasks are within the ai scope of abilities, and they feel so streamline and easy

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's lack of cultural nuance
nodes.grounding_span: it is really hard to get ai to understand culture

nodes.label: Ambivalence toward AI for quizzes
nodes.grounding_span: I have never actually felt satisfied with the options I was given

nodes.label: Optimism about future AI personalization
nodes.grounding_span: Hopefully as Ai evolves, there would be opportunity to create more indepth and customisable worksheets

nodes.label: Iterative prompt refinement
nodes.grounding_span: I tried to rework the prompt a couple of times

nodes.label: Domain-specific reliance on human networks for cultural nuance
nodes.grounding_span: In order to get cultural context I had to actually ask my native friends
